messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.17.1010, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - When configuring the same BGP neighbor under both IPv4 and IPv6 address families on a router, if a maximum prefix limit is set on one address family (like IPv4), exceeding that limit can potentially impact the BGP session for the other address family (IPv6) as well.

description: Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur after resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

description: Symptom: OSPF default route in area 0 points to a stub router instead of ASBR via backbone area. Scenario: This issue can occur when there are two paths to the ASBR that generated the external route - one via stub area and another via non-stub area. The external route should get installed with the path reachable through the non-stub area, but the path via stub area gets installed, leading to a forwarding loop. Workaround: Enable rfc1583_compatability under the router ospf context.

description: Symptom: After a VSX failover (following an MCLAG L2 port unshut event), EVPN MAC+IP Type-2 (RT2) routes for SVIs in the default VRF were not re-advertised to remote VTEP peers. This causes remote peers to lose reachability to hosts on those SVIs. Scenario: This issue occurs when the ARP/neighbor scan in ms_idl_bd_send_all_mac_ip_add_update() and ms_idl_bd_send_all_mac_ip_delete() is gated on port_row->vrf != NULL. For SVIs in the default VRF, the VRF column of the port row in OVSDB is NULL. This causes the entire neighbor scan loop to be silently skipped, preventing RT2 re-advertisement. Workaround: The VRF null check has been moved from the outer port-row check into the per-neighbor loop. Each neighbor entry is now individually guarded by !vrf_status_ready(nbr_row->vrf), which correctly handles default-VRF SVIs since nbr_row->vrf is non-NULL even for default-VRF neighbors. MAC+IP RT2 routes are now properly re-advertised after SVI recovery following a VSX failover event.

symptom: After a VSX failover (following an MCLAG L2 port unshut event), EVPN MAC+IP Type-2 (RT2) routes for SVIs in the default VRF were not re-advertised to remote VTEP peers. This causes remote peers to lose reachability to hosts on those SVIs.
scenario: This issue occurs when the ARP/neighbor scan in ms_idl_bd_send_all_mac_ip_add_update() and ms_idl_bd_send_all_mac_ip_delete() is gated on port_row->vrf != NULL. For SVIs in the default VRF, the VRF column of the port row in OVSDB is NULL. This causes the entire neighbor scan loop to be silently skipped, preventing RT2 re-advertisement.
workaround: The VRF null check has been moved from the outer port-row check into the per-neighbor loop. Each neighbor entry is now individually guarded by !vrf_status_ready(nbr_row->vrf), which correctly handles default-VRF SVIs since nbr_row->vrf is non-NULL even for default-VRF neighbors. MAC+IP RT2 routes are now properly re-advertised after SVI recovery following a VSX failover event.

Each neighbor entry is now individually guarded by !vrf_status_ready(nbr_row->vrf), which correctly handles default-VRF SVIs since nbr_row->vrf is non-NULL even for default-VRF neighbors. MAC+IP RT2 routes are now properly re-advertised after SVI recovery following a VSX failover event.

description: Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.
